title: Where Teachers Are Paid Like Professionals
alt_text: Data visualization: teacher salary ratios vs PISA educational outcomes
slides:
Luxembourg pays teachers 198% of the average salary. The US pays 64%.

That gap — not just the number, but what it signals about how a society values the profession — shows up directly in outcomes. 🧵

The correlation between teacher pay and PISA scores is real.

But the outliers are more interesting. Singapore: top of the world. Poland: low pay, strong outcomes. Luxembourg: highest pay, middle results.

Pay is one signal. Status is the actual variable.

Finland accepts fewer than 10% of teacher training applicants. Singapore recruits from the top third of graduates.

The US accepts the majority. The signal sent: this is not a prestige career.

The data reflects that signal back.

Data: OECD EAG 2023 · PISA 2022 | #education #teachers #dataviz
sources: OECD Education at a Glance 2023 · PISA 2022
slides_v2_reference: Luxembourg pays teachers 198% of the average salary. The US pays 64%.

Poland: 635 hours of school, PISA 516. The US: 1,016 hours, PISA 497.

More hours. Worse outcomes. The constraint isn't time.

Finland accepts fewer than 10% of teacher training applicants. Teaching is harder to get into than medicine.

The US accepts the majority. The profession reflects that.
